account_plan_attestation: Starter paid subscription, verified through the account API on 2026-07-14
commercial_use_basis: generated during the account owner's paid Starter subscription; use is subject to the ElevenLabs terms captured by the raw candidate metadata

cues.prompt: A perfectly seamless close loop of one small lunar lander main rocket thruster firing steadily: dense low combustion rumble, turbulent exhaust pressure, subtle mechanical pump pulse, and stable continuous power. Realistic dry exterior spacecraft sound. No ignition or shutdown, no launch sequence, no alarms, no voice, no music, no ambience, no changing thrust level.

A perfectly seamless close loop of one small lunar lander attitude-control jet firing steadily: focused high-pressure gas hiss, compact turbulent core, faint valve vibration, and stable continuous output. Realistic dry exterior spacecraft sound. No ignition or shutdown, no main-engine bass, no alarms, no voice, no music, no changing thrust level.

One isolated realistic small metal lunar lander crashing hard onto bare rock: violent aluminum frame crunch, one pressure-tank pop, deep ground impact, landing-leg snap, and scattered light metal debris. Dry close exterior recording, one crash only. No alarm, no voice, no giant explosion, no fireball, no music, no long reverb.

One successful lunar touchdown confirmation: a subtle metal landing-leg compression thump followed immediately by four calm ascending analog spacecraft tones. Clean restrained control-panel sound, compact and original. No voice, no words, no applause, no music bed, no engine noise, no long reverb.

One isolated spacecraft instrument confirmation beep: a tiny relay click and one short warm analog sine chirp rising slightly in pitch. Precise, dry, soft-edged, and readable. Exactly one beep. No second pulse, no warning alarm, no voice, no music, no ambience, no harsh digital distortion.

One compact spacecraft hazard warning: exactly two low analog electronic pulses descending in pitch, urgent and readable but not piercing. Dry control-panel sound with a clean cutoff. No siren, no voice, no words, no music, no ambience, no additional pulses, no long reverb.

One isolated spacecraft computer menu move: a precise miniature relay click followed by a tiny warm electronic blip. Extremely short, restrained, dry, and original. Exactly one cue. No warning alarm, no second beep, no voice, no music, no ambience, no harsh high frequency.

mastering.description: one-shots use onset trim, exact duration fit, equal-power mono downmix, DC removal, 24 Hz high-pass, short edge fades, and 4x reconstructed-peak gain staging; loops use stable-window selection, equal-value smoothstep boundary preparation, exact duration fit, and reconstructed-peak gain staging

clap.purpose: candidate triage only; not a substitute for listening
clap.reproducibility: normalized candidate score arrays identical across two independent runs

selection.pilot_policy: retain any user-auditioned pilot candidates recorded for this pack
selection.quality_priority: new packs rank semantic and signal validity ahead of waveform diversity; correlation remains recorded for audit

sfx_sublicensing_opt_out: not observable through the API; managed with the Disable control on the ElevenLabs Sound Effects product page
standalone_distribution: intended only as bundled terminal-lander game assets, not a standalone SFX library
